name: Maps

values.name: defaultModel
values.comment: The default model is a map zoomed into Sydney, Australia with no markers.

values.name: mapView
values.comment:
Transforms the Maps HTML view into an arbitrary HTML view.
Requires a function that can transform `Maps.Msg`s into `msg`s.

    import Html
    import Html.Event exposing (onClick)
    import Maps

    type MyMsg = Click | MapsMsg Maps.Msg

    ...

    view msg model =
      Html.div
        []
        [ Maps.view model.map |> Maps.mapView MapsMsg
        , Html.button [ onClick Click ] [ Html.text "Click!" ]
        ]
values.type: (Maps.Msg msg -> msg) -> Html.Html (Maps.Msg msg) -> Html.Html msg

values.name: update
values.type: Maps.Msg msg -> Maps.Model msg -> ( Maps.Model msg, Platform.Cmd.Cmd (Maps.Msg msg) )

values.name: updateAreas
values.comment:
Change the areas inside of the model

For example, add some areas to the map:

    import Maps.Area exposing (Area, create, withRotation, withBorder, withFill)
    import Maps.Geo exposing (latLng)

    let
      areas =
        [ ( latLng 54.797 9.425, latLng 54.794 9.435 )
        , ( latLng 54.782 9.436, latLng 54.78 9.438 )
        ]
        |> List.map (\( topLeft, bottomRight ) ->
            create topLeft bottomRight
              |> withRotation 30
              |> withBorder { width = 2, color = "blue" }
              |> withFill "rgba(0, 128, 255, 0.4)"
          )
    in
      model
      |> updateAreas (\_ -> areas)
values.type: (List.List Maps.Area.Area -> List.List Maps.Area.Area) -> Maps.Model msg -> Maps.Model msg

values.name: updateMap
values.comment:
Change the map inside of a model.

For example, set the width/height of a map and zoom into Seoul, South Korea:

    import Maps.Geo
    import Maps.Map as Map

    let
      seoul = Maps.Geo.latLng 37.532600 127.024612
    in
      model
      |> updateMap (Map.setHeight 600)
      |> updateMap (Map.setWidth 1000)
      |> updateMap (Map.viewBounds <| Maps.Geo.centeredBounds 10 seoul)

See [Maps.Map](./Maps-Map) for documentation of the Map functions.

values.name: updateMarkers
values.comment:
Change the markers inside of the model

For example, add markers for some Sydney attractions and then another marker for the city center:

    import Maps.Geo
    import Maps.Marker as Marker

    let
      attractions =
        List.map (uncurry Maps.Geo.latLng)
          [ (-33.852324, 151.210819)
          , (-33.856872, 151.215239)
          , (-33.870397, 151.208835)
          ]
      sydney = Maps.Geo.latLng -33.865143 151.209900
    in
      model
      |> updateMarkers (\markers -> List.map Marker.create attractions ++ markers)
      |> updateMarkers ((::) (Marker.create sydney))

See [Maps.Marker](./Maps-Marker) for documentation of the Marker functions.
values.type: (List.List (Maps.Marker.Marker msg) -> List.List (Maps.Marker.Marker msg)) -> Maps.Model msg -> Maps.Model msg

values.name: updateOptions
values.comment:
Change the options inside of the model
For example, disable dragging and zooming of the map:

    import Maps.Internal.Options exposing (Options)

    model
    |> updateOptions (\o -> { o | enableDrag = False, enablePinch = False, enableZoom = False })

name: Maps.Area

aliases.name: Area
aliases.comment: Represents a rectangular polygonal area on the map.

aliases.name: AreaStyle
aliases.comment: Represents the style of an area, including fill color and optional border.

values.name: create
values.comment:
Create a rectangular area from a top-left and bottom-right coordinate,
with a rotation in degrees (clockwise, around the center of the rectangle).

    import Maps.Area
    import Maps.Geo exposing (latLng)

    myArea =
        Maps.Area.create
            -- top-left
            (latLng 52.52 13.38)
            -- bottom-right
            (latLng 52.51 13.39)
            -- rotation in degrees
            30

values.name: withBorder
values.comment:
Set the border of an area with a specified width and color.

    import Maps.Area exposing (Area, withBorder)

    myArea : Area
    myArea =
        Maps.Area.create
            (Maps.Geo.latLng 52.52 13.38)
            (Maps.Geo.latLng 52.51 13.39)
            |> withBorder { width = 2, color = "blue" }
values.type: { width : Basics.Int, color : String.String } -> Maps.Area.Area -> Maps.Area.Area

values.name: withFill
values.comment:
Set the fill color of an area.

    import Maps.Area exposing (Area, withFill)

    myArea : Area
    myArea =
        Maps.Area.create
            (Maps.Geo.latLng 52.52 13.38)
            (Maps.Geo.latLng 52.51 13.39)
            |> withFill "rgba(0, 128, 255, 0.4)"

values.name: withRotation
values.comment:
Set the rotation of an area in degrees (clockwise, around the center of the rectangle).

    import Maps.Area exposing (Area, withRotation)

    myArea : Area
    myArea =
        Maps.Area.create
            (Maps.Geo.latLng 52.52 13.38)
            (Maps.Geo.latLng 52.51 13.39)
            |> withRotation 45

name: Maps.Convert

aliases.name: MapSizes
aliases.comment:
The size properties of a map.
The conversion functions that require this type can just be passed a map type.
aliases.type: { a | tileSize : Basics.Float, zoom : Maps.Internal.Screen.ZoomLevel, width : Basics.Float, height : Basics.Float, center : Maps.Geo.LatLng }

values.name: latLngToScreenOffset
values.comment:
Take latitude/longitude anc convert it to an offset from the top left of the map.
Note that it requires the dimensions of the map (or the map itself) to calculate this conversion.

values.name: screenOffsetToLatLng
values.comment:
Take an offset from the top left of the map and convert it to a latitude/longitude.
Note that it requires the dimensions of the map (or the map itself) to calculate this conversion.

name: Maps.Geo

aliases.name: Bounds
LatLng

values.name: bounds
values.comment:
Create a Bounds using a northeast and southwest point.

For example, the bounds of Ecuador

    ecuador =
        bounds
            { northEast = latLng 1.4284875 -75.188794
            , southWest = latLng -5.0143511 -81.08498089999999
            }

values.name: centeredBounds
values.comment:
Create a Bounds centered on a location with a given zoom level.

For example, zoomed into the streets of Baku, Azerbaijan:

    baku =
        centeredBounds
            14
            (latLng 40.409264 49.867092)

values.name: latLng
values.comment:
Create a LatLng.

For example:

    latLng 45 -175

name: Maps.Map

aliases.name: Map
aliases.comment:
The map type contains all the information necessary to display a map on the screen.
The map type is opaque, so use the functions in this module to maniuplate the map.

values.name: move
values.comment:
Move the map a given number of pixels in the x and y dimensions.

For example, up 10px and right 10px:

    map
        |> move { x = 10, y = -10 }

values.name: moveTo
values.comment:
Move the center of the map to a specific location.

For example, move the map to Shanghai:

    map
        |> moveTo (Maps.Geo.LatLng 31.267401 121.522179)

values.name: setHeight
values.comment: Set the height, as displayed on the screen, of a given Map.

values.name: setTileServer
values.comment:
Set the tile server.

The format is a URL with {x} {y} {z} placeholders for the x, y and zoom coordinates.

values.name: setTileSize
values.comment:
Set the width/height in px of a tile.
Note, this is dependent on the tile server, and the default of 256px is almost always correct.

values.name: setWidth
values.comment: Set the width, as displayed on the screen, of a given Map.

values.name: setZoom
values.comment:
Sets the zoom to a specific level

For example, zoom all the way out

    map
        |> zoom 0

Or all the way in:

    map
        |> zoom 19

values.name: viewBounds
values.comment:
Move and zoom the map to cover given the bounds.

For example, view the bounds of Madagascar:

    let
        madagascar =
            Maps.Geo.bounds
                { northEast = Maps.Geo.latLng -11.9519639 50.48377989999999
                , southWest = Maps.Geo.latLng -25.6065157 43.1851395
                }
    in
    map
        |> viewBounds madagascar

values.name: zoom
values.comment:
Zoom into the center of the map.

For example zoom out two levels:

    map
        |> zoom -2

values.name: zoomTo
values.comment:
Zoom into an x,y co-ordinate on the map.

For example, zoom into the top left corner of the map:

    map
        |> zoom 1 { x = 0, y = 0 }

name: Maps.Marker

aliases.name: Marker
aliases.comment:
There are currently two types of marker:

  - A default marker
  - A custom HTML marker

values.name: create
values.comment:
Create a default style of marker at the given latitude/longitude.

    import Maps.Geo
    import Maps.Marker

    newYork =
        Maps.Geo.latLng 40.73061 -73.935242

    newYorkMarker =
        Maps.Marker.create newYork

values.name: createCustom
values.comment: Create a custom HTML marker at the given latitude/longitude.
